---
title: "Gaussian mixture models"
output: html_notebook
---

# Functions 

```{r}
# Initialise the model 
initialise = function(x, k) {
  
  # Initialise
  model.obj = list(data = x,
                   class.prob = NULL,
                   class.means = NULL,
                   class.var = NULL)
  
  # Assign each data point randomly to be 
  # generated from a distribution (hard assignment)
  randomAssign = function(x, k){
    x[sample(1:k, 1)] = 1
    return(x)
  }
  class.prob = matrix(rep(0,length(model.obj$data) * k), nrow = length(model.obj$data))
  model.obj$class.prob = t(apply(class.prob, 1, randomAssign, k = k))
  
  
  model.obj$class.means = t(as.matrix(model.obj$data)) %*% as.matrix(model.obj$class.prob) /
  t(t(as.matrix(model.obj$class.prob)) %*% as.matrix(model.obj$class.prob) %*% rep(1, k))
  
  
  #sample(1:k, length(model.obj$data))
  return(model.obj)
}

```


```{r}
rep(1, 2)
```


```{r}
x = c(1,2,3,4,2,1,5)
k = 2
```

```{r}
initialise(x, k)
```
